name: Prefer Exceptions to Returning Error Codes
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 3: Functions
problem: Returning error codes from command functions forces callers to check and handle errors immediately with deeply nested if-else chains, mixing happy-path logic with error handling and violating the separation of concerns.
steps: Replace return-code error handling with exceptions.
Wrap the happy-path operations in a try block and handle errors in a catch block.
Extract the try and catch bodies into separate named functions.
Ensure error handling is the only thing the enclosing function does.
Avoid the Error.java dependency-magnet anti-pattern by using standard exception hierarchies.
